location.name: Choose
location.label: NB: States may be misleading if shielded. The shield simply intervenes by altering the acceleration once it has been chosen by an agent. For this reason, the car might be in the "PositiveAcc" state even though it is at that time reversing.

(forall (i : int[0, fleetSize - 1]) velocity[i]' == acceleration[i]) && (forall (j : int[0, fleetSize - 2]) distance[j]' == velocity[j] - velocity[j + 1]) && (forall (j : int[0, fleetSize - 2]) D[j]' == distance[j])

plot.title: Probability Density Distribution
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=431.5, bucket count=32 Runs: 1000 in total, 1000 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [1979.1, 15787] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 8401.3 ± 164.41 (95% CI)

plot.title: Probability Distribution
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=431.5, bucket count=32 Runs: 1000 in total, 1000 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [1979.1, 15787] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 8401.3 ± 164.41 (95% CI)

plot.title: Cumulative Probability Distribution
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=431.5, bucket count=32 Runs: 1000 in total, 1000 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [1979.1, 15787] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 8401.3 ± 164.41 (95% CI)

plot.title: Cumulative Probability Confidence Intervals
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=431.5, bucket count=32 Runs: 1000 in total, 1000 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [1979.1, 15787] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 8401.3 ± 164.41 (95% CI)

plot.title: Frequency Histogram
plot.comment: Parameters: α=0.05, ε=0.05, bucket width=431.5, bucket count=32 Runs: 1000 in total, 1000 (100%) displayed, 0 (0%) remaining Span of displayed sample: [1979.1, 15787] Mean estimate of displayed sample: 8401.3 ± 164.41 (95% CI)

query.formula: Pr[<=100;1000](<> forall (i : int[0, fleetSize - 2]) (distance[i] < minDistance || distance[i] > maxDistance))
